analysis.reasoning: Core message: The EU is dispatching newly approved COVID-19 vaccines to all 27 member states for coordinated EU Vaccination Days on Dec 27–29. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – implied operational success and milestone but not about history or overcoming past traumas; only weakly suggested, so not strong enough. EU2 (EU is united) – explicitly references delivery to all 27 countries and coordinated vaccination days, showcasing unity and solidarity; supported. EU3 (useful for its citizens) – demonstrates a practical, timely EU action (approval, distribution, coordinated rollout) improving public health; supported. EU4 (avant-garde) – no claim of pioneering leadership or regulatory firsts; not supported. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – not about rights/democracy; not supported. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – focus is internal EU rollout, not global actions; not supported. Conclusion: Narratives EU2 and EU3 are promoted.
